subject: skills/leash_for_hooks/lib/data_point.py — provenance shape
principal: The data point's `provenance` block (collected_at, source_state, collector_pointer) was authored from CLAUDE.md's prose. It does not conform to W3C PROV-DM (the published standard for provenance metadata) and the field names/structure are local invention.
interest: Any external consumer of our datasets cannot read provenance without writing a custom parser. The 'engineering, not theatre' claim of 0.4 is weaker because the most load-bearing metadata field — provenance — is the part most disconnected from real standards.
payoff: Refactor lib/data_point.py to produce PROV-JSON-conformant provenance per https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-dm/ and https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-json/. Map (collected_at, source_state, collector_pointer) to (prov:wasGeneratedAtTime, prov:wasDerivedFrom, prov:wasAttributedTo). Validator uses the prov-python library or equivalent.
re_trigger: second consumer of bedrock data appears (e.g., a third-party tool wants to read our provenance) OR a foundation 0.4 grading event re-opens the bedrock for grounding

closure.evidence: commit on issue-1 branch: prov>=2.0 added to pyproject.toml; data_point.py provenance block refactored to prov:wasAttributedTo / prov:wasDerivedFrom / prov:wasGeneratedAtTime per W3C PROV-DM; prov-python used in _validate_prov_block() to round-trip the block through a ProvDocument (ProvGeneration + ProvDerivation + ProvAttribution); verify still 19/19 green. Commit SHA: 141f618
